page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c9a.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, utilizing the AMD EPYC 9T25 processor running at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. With a single thread per core configuration, this instance provides dedicated compute resources without hyperthreading, ensuring predictable execution performance for CPU-bound workloads.

The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This allocation profile positions the server within the compute-optimized category, prioritizing processing power over memory density. It does not include local NVMe storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.c9a.2xlarge offers a balanced resource profile for workloads requiring dedicated CPU execution without the overhead of hyperthreading. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs reduces unnecessary hardware costs, making it a cost-effective choice for pure compute tasks. This hardware configuration is suitable for workloads such as batch processing, web serving, distributed analytics, and lightweight compilation tasks where predictable CPU performance is critical.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c9a.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9T25 processor running at 2.7 GHz. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a single thread per core configuration for predictable, dedicated CPU performance. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, offering a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, making it a streamlined option for pure compute workloads. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for tasks that do not require massive memory footprints or local disk I/O, such as batch processing, web serving, and distributed compute nodes.
